title: recall(query, scope) on pgvector (hybrid + RRF)
userStory: As a consumer app, I want recall(query, scope) so that I can look up the most relevant chunks for a query, optionally within a domain.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /recall?q=&scope= runs semantic (pgvector cosine <=>) and lexical (tsvector) searches and fuses them with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, scoped by sources.path LIKE scope%
Returns a ranked list of Chunk{heading, text, score, path}
With the Target-role page ingested, recall('what locations is Steve open to') returns that page's chunk
scope filters correctly (a non-matching scope returns nothing); empty or garbage query is handled gracefully
Verify: covered by the US-010 smoke (a relevant query returns the chunk)
